title: fagara kauaensis
intro: Fagara kauaensis, also known as Hawaiian prickly ash or Kawa 'Ula, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is a shrub or small tree native to Hawaii and the Pacific Islands, with medicinal properties attributed to its bark, leaves, and roots. The plant contains compounds such as coumarin and limonene, which have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bark can be used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and salves, which may help to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
